resource_notes.change: Only the all64 task raster changes. Gate/up and down each retain their original CTA count and two launches, but use a one-dimensional pid grid mapped back to (bx, by) with the standard TileOps grouped-M formula and GROUP_SIZE_M=2.
resource_notes.small_shape: hidden=256, intermediate=256, experts=4, valid=568, seed=81394: compiled, allclose=true, max_abs_error=7.62939453125e-06, mean_ms=0.11434666315714519.
resource_notes.formal_protocol: All three points used seed=81394, warmup=10, iters=100, --check and passed allclose.
resource_notes.launches: Two launches, unchanged from baseline. Total gate/up and down CTA counts are unchanged for every shape.
resource_notes.resources: Tile, thread count, pack factors, shared-memory buffers, accumulator fragments, K loops, swizzle calls, and mathematics are byte-for-byte equivalent inside each mapped CTA. The compiler emitted no register or spill diagnostics.
resource_notes.mechanism: GROUP_SIZE_M=2 interleaves two M tiles per N tile in the one-dimensional launch order. On C500 this conflicts with the effective locality/scheduling of the original two-dimensional grid and its swizzle, causing consistent 28-34% regressions.
resource_notes.random_groups: Not run because every formal point regressed and the >=2% retention gate was already impossible.
